title: Rank-Diverse Ensemble
summary: ROC AUC rewards ordering, not calibrated probability accuracy.
rationale: ROC AUC rewards ordering, not calibrated probability accuracy. A blend of models with different inductive biases may improve ranking beyond the saturated engineered-feature LightGBM/AutoGluon style baseline: native categorical CatBoost, monotone-constrained LightGBM, and LightGBM rank_xendcg/lambdarank grouped by race.
implementation_hint: Train CatBoost with raw Driver/Race/Compound and key interactions as categorical features, LightGBM binary with monotone constraints on TyreLife_ExpectedLife_Ratio/RaceProgress where sensible, and LGBMRanker with query groups Year_Race or Year_Race_Stint. Blend OOF rank-normalized predictions; use sigmoid calibration only if a submission requires smoother probabilities, since monotone calibration should preserve AUC ranking.
expected_effect: Small but testable AUC lift from model diversity and rank-focused optimization.
risk: Rank objectives need careful group construction and may underperform if positives are too sparse per query; isotonic calibration can introduce ties and hurt AUC.
